AVIATA AT SAINT LUCIE

CCN: 105257 · FORT PIERCE, FL 34950 · St. Lucie County

Overview

Address
611 S 13TH ST, FORT PIERCE, FL 34950
Phone
7724645262
Certified beds
171

125 (73% of beds filled)
Ownership
For-profit LLC
Provider type

Medicare/Medicaid since
1972-09-09
Setting
Urban
1 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 1/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 1/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 3/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 3/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 1/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 3.84 3.53 4.20 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.88 0.62 0.96 ≥ 0.55
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.45 0.78 0.49 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 2.51 2.13 2.75 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.33 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.00 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
3.64
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.75
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
3.99
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
New facility — insufficient data.
RN turnover
New facility — insufficient data.
Administrators departed
1
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.25
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
0.91
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-03-20 24 22 2 100 1 100
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2023-11-17 28 15 14 239 1 239
Total weighted health score
134.8
lower is better

Deficiencies (65)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

62 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
3 J/K/L Immediate jeopardy
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2025-07-29 2025-08-29
0697 Provide safe, appropriate pain management for a resident who requires such services. D 2025-07-29 2025-08-29
0561 Honor the resident's right to and the facility must promote and facilitate resident self-determination through support of resident choice. D 2025-03-20 2025-04-20
0582 Give residents notice of Medicaid/Medicare coverage and potential liability for services not covered. D 2025-03-20 2025-04-20
0583 Keep residents' personal and medical records private and confidential. D 2025-03-20 2025-04-20
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. D 2025-03-20 2025-04-20
0645 PASARR screening for Mental disorders or Intellectual Disabilities D 2025-03-20 2025-04-20
0655 Create and put into place a plan for meeting the resident's most immediate needs within 48 hours of being admitted D 2025-03-20 2025-04-20
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2025-03-20 2025-04-20
0679 Provide activities to meet all resident's needs. D 2025-03-20 2025-04-20
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2025-03-20 2025-04-20
0690 Provide appropriate care for residents who are continent or incontinent of bowel/bladder, appropriate catheter care, and appropriate care to prevent urinary tract infections. D 2025-03-20 2025-04-20
0692 Provide enough food/fluids to maintain a resident's health. D 2025-03-20 2025-04-20
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2025-03-20 2025-04-20
0697 Provide safe, appropriate pain management for a resident who requires such services. D 2025-03-20 2025-04-20
0698 Provide safe, appropriate dialysis care/services for a resident who requires such services. D 2025-03-20 2025-04-20
0699 Provide care or services that was trauma informed and/or culturally competent. D 2025-03-20 2025-04-20
0725 Provide enough nursing staff every day to meet the needs of every resident; and have a licensed nurse in charge on each shift. D 2025-03-20 2025-04-20
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. D 2025-03-20 2025-04-20
0757 Ensure each resident’s drug regimen must be free from unnecessary drugs. D 2025-03-20 2025-04-20
0759 Ensure medication error rates are not 5 percent or greater. D 2025-03-20 2025-04-20
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2025-03-20 2025-04-20
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2025-03-20 2025-04-20
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2025-03-20 2025-04-20
0658 Ensure services provided by the nursing facility meet professional standards of quality. E 2024-11-04 2024-12-04
0675 Honor each resident's preferences, choices, values and beliefs. D 2024-11-04 2024-12-04
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2024-11-04 2024-12-04
0694 Provide for the safe, appropriate administration of IV fluids for a resident when needed. D 2024-11-04 2024-12-04
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. J 2024-11-01 2024-08-23
0698 Provide safe, appropriate dialysis care/services for a resident who requires such services. J 2024-11-01 2024-08-23
0806 Ensure each resident receives and the facility provides food that accommodates resident allergies, intolerances, and preferences, as well as appealing options. D 2024-06-26 2024-07-23
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. D 2024-06-26 2024-07-23
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. D 2024-04-10 2024-05-03
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. J 2024-04-10 2024-05-03
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. E 2023-11-17 2023-12-17
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. D 2023-11-17 2023-12-17
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. E 2023-11-17 2023-12-17
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. D 2023-11-17 2023-12-17
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. D 2023-11-17 2023-12-17
0636 Assess the resident completely in a timely manner when first admitted, and then periodically, at least every 12 months. D 2023-11-17 2023-12-17
0645 PASARR screening for Mental disorders or Intellectual Disabilities D 2023-11-17 2023-12-17
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. D 2023-11-17 2023-12-17
0693 Ensure that feeding tubes are not used unless there is a medical reason and the resident agrees; and provide appropriate care for a resident with a feeding tube. D 2023-11-17 2023-12-17
0759 Ensure medication error rates are not 5 percent or greater. D 2023-11-17 2023-12-17
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2023-11-17 2023-12-17
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2023-11-17 2023-12-17
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2023-11-17 2023-12-17
0881 Implement a program that monitors antibiotic use. D 2023-11-17 2023-12-17
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2023-11-17 2023-12-17
0561 Honor the resident's right to and the facility must promote and facilitate resident self-determination through support of resident choice. D 2023-04-20 2023-05-12
